summary: Polycomb group proteins primarily function in epigenetic regulation via histone modification and gene silencing but do not have documented roles in forming chromatin loops; while they may associate with loop anchor sites, this correlation does not equate to active participation in loop establishment. The available literature lacks clear support linking PcG activity to mechanical chromosome looping mechanics.
